revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横穴式石室]][[ko:굴식 돌방무덤]][[zh:橫穴式石室]]
{{distinguish|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|ja|横穴墓|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|qq=}}[[File:Monjuin Nishi Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Monju-in Nishi Kofun|en|Monju-in Nishi Kofun|qid=Q24894422|lt=Monju-in Nishi Kofun|1=Monju-in Nishi Kofun}}'s corridor-style stone chamber]] '''Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (横穴式石室) refers to a burial style originating in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q29520}} during the {{ill|Western Han dynasty|ja|前漢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Han dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Western Han|qid=Q1072949}} and widely constructed in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. This burial system features a passage ({{ill|sendō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sendō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}) leading to an inner burial chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gen-shitsu|qq=}}), excavated horizontally and reinforced with stone.<ref>{{Kotobank|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|{{ill|Britannica International Encyclopedia|ja|ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Britannica International Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Britannica International Encyclopedia|qid=Q109359966}}|qq=}}</ref> It traces its origins to the brick-chambered tombs ({{ill|sen-shitsu-bo|ja|塼室墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sen-shitsu-bo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) popularized across {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q29520}} during the mid-Western Han period. They contrast with {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} == Overview == The horizontal stone chamber is a universally observed architectural feature of Kofuns across regions such as {{ill|Europe|en|Europe|qid=Q46|lt=Europe|1=Europe}} (e.g., passage graves in {{ill|United Kingdom|en|United Kingdom|qid=Q145|lt=United Kingdom|1=United Kingdom}}) and {{ill|India|en|India|qid=Q668|lt=India|1=India}}. In Japan, it specifically denotes tomb structures developed during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} under the influence of Chinese brick-chambered tombs ({{ill|sen-kaku-bo|ja|塼槨墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sen-kaku-bo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and was further adapted by {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} and other peninsular kingdoms before spreading throughout Japan. In China, horizontal burial chambers constructed from brick were developed extensively during the Han dynasty.<ref>{{Kotobank|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|{{ill|MyPedia Encyclopedia|ja|マイペディア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MyPedia Encyclopedia|1=My Pedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11339782}}|qq=}}</ref> These burial customs were transmitted through the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|qid=Q483134}}, particularly influenced by {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|qid=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}, and reached Japan by the 5th century, becoming widespread in the 6th and 7th centuries. Iconic examples include the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|qid=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, which features megalithic construction (though the mound has been lost), and chambered tombs employing cut stone or dressed stone arranged in a {{ill|gonomedzumi|ja|互目積|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gonomedzumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (checkerboard) style, reminiscent of Chinese brick-chambered tombs. == Tombs in Japan == Horizontal stone chambers appeared in Japan around the late 4th century, starting in northern {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|qid=Q13987|lt=Kyūshū|1=Kyushu}}, before spreading eastward: * '''Late 4th century:''' Early examples include {{ill|Rōji Kofun|en|Rōji Kofun|qid=Q18459458|lt=Rōji Kofun|1=Rōji Kofun}} and {{ill|Kuwasaki Kofun|ja|鍬崎古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasaki Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}, both keyhole-shaped tumuli measuring approximately 90m and 62m in length. Multiple burial chambers were constructed in the rear and front portions of the tumuli. * '''Early 5th century:''' Notable examples include {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukuma-yama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka and {{ill|Yokota Shimo Kofun|ja|横田下古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokota Shimo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|qid=Q738275|lt=Karatsu, Saga|1=Karatsu, Saga}}, featuring finely shaped stone chambers and multiple burial facilities.<ref>Shigeru Sata, "The Eve of Hizen Province," in {{ill|History of Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県の歴史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Saga Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2002), p. 29.</ref> * '''Mid- to late 5th century:''' Regional styles emerged, such as the {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|lt=Sekijinzan Kofun|lt=Sekijin-yama Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin-yama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa, Fukuoka|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485375}} and {{ill|Eta Funayama Kofun|en|Eta Funayama Kofun|qid=Q11259227|lt=Eda-funayama Kofun|1=Eta Funayama Kofun}} in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. == Korea == Horizontal stone chambers are commonly found in the so-called {{ill|Lelang tomb clusters|ja|楽浪古墳群|ko|낙랑 고분|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lelang tomb clusters|qq=}} in present-day {{ill|Pyongyang|ja|平壌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pyongyang|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平壌市|1=Pyongyang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18808}} and {{ill|Hwanghae Province|en|Hwanghae Province|qid=Q698214|lt=Hwanghae Province|1=Hwanghae Province}}. These tombs emphasize the significant influence of {{ill|Han dynasty|ja|漢王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 漢|1=Han dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7209}} culture on {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|qid=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}.<ref name="Pai127">{{Cite book|title=Constructing "Korean" Origins: A Critical Review of Archaeology, Historiography, and Racial Myth in Korean State Formation Theories|author=Hyung Il Pai|publisher=Harvard University Asia Center|year=2000|pages=127-129|qq=}}</ref> == Examples == * {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|qid=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} (Nara): A megalithic two-sided chamber believed to house a gabled Sarcophagus. * {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}} (Nara): The largest chamber, estimated to use stones weighing up to 100 tons. * {{ill|Monju-in Nishi Kofun|en|Monju-in Nishi Kofun|qid=Q24894422|lt=Monju-in Nishi Kofun|1=Monju-in Nishi Kofun}} (Nara): A finely crafted chamber considered the apex of horizontal stone chamber design.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} * {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Passage grave|en|Passage grave|qid=Q1426772|lt=Passage grave|1=Passage grave}} ==References== <references />
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|横穴式石室|version=103805678|comment=Imported full ja history}}
